description: 5G core NF drill-down — registration procedure step by step, per-NF resource usage, policy, and logs

panels.description: Step 1 of UE attach. The UE sends a Registration Request (NAS) to the AMF via gNB. AMF processes it and returns Accept or Reject.
panels.title: Initial Registration (RM)

panels.description: UE re-registers after moving between cells or TACs. High mob reg rate means UE is moving frequently through different tracking areas.
panels.title: Mobility Registration (RM)

panels.description: UE re-registers periodically to signal it is still reachable. This keeps the AMF context alive. Normal behaviour in an idle but attached UE.
panels.title: Periodic Registration (RM)

panels.description: AMF triggers authentication after Registration Request. Auth Req count should equal Reg Req count. Fails = wrong KI/OP or SIM mismatch. Rejects = network decision to deny.
panels.title: Authentication (5G-AKA)

panels.description: AMF pages an idle UE to deliver downlink data. High paging rate with low success indicates UE is not reachable — possible RAN or UE coverage issue.
panels.title: Paging (MM)

panels.description: PDU session creation request and success. One PDU session per UE per DNN. The session gives the UE its IP address and QoS policy.
panels.title: PDU Session Lifecycle

panels.description: SMF uses PFCP over N4 to program packet forwarding rules in the UPF. N4 Estab = tunnel setup. N4 Reports = UPF reporting usage back to SMF.
panels.title: N4 Interface — SMF ↔ UPF (PFCP)

panels.description: Actual user data packets traversing the N3 GTP tunnel. Uplink = UE sending data. Downlink = UE receiving data. Zero here means no active data traffic even if session is established.
panels.title: N3 GTP Packet Rate — gNB ↔ UPF

panels.description: Container-level byte counters for the UPF. Includes N3 (gNB), N4 (SMF) and N6 (internet) interface traffic combined.
panels.title: UPF Container Network Throughput

panels.description: PCF creates policy associations for UE Access Management (AM) and Session Management (SM). AM Policy controls mobility behaviour. SM Policy controls session QoS and charging.
panels.title: Policy Associations — AM & SM

title: 5G Core Detail
